intro:
Facebook Dating has one obvious advantage: it already knows everything about you. Your social graph, your groups, your events, your friends — all available as compatibility signals before you've answered a single question. For users already deep in the Facebook ecosystem, that integration is genuinely useful.

But Facebook Dating also inherits everything people dislike about Facebook: data privacy concerns, the feeling of being profiled, a social graph that might know your dating activity, and a company whose relationship with user trust has been tested repeatedly. Embir is privacy-first dating — your data is used to find compatible people, not to target you with ads or feed a social media algorithm.

comparisonTable.embir: Standalone app — no social media integration

comparisonTable.competitor: Facebook account required, no extra verification
Dating data may inform Meta ad targeting
Free — but your data is the product
comparisonTable.embir: core connection features are free, transparent freemium model

sections.title: Privacy — your dating life vs Meta's data model
sections.content:
Facebook Dating is free because you're not the customer — you're the product. Your dating activity, your matches, your conversations, and your preferences become data points that enrich Meta's advertising profile of you. Facebook explicitly says dating information won't be used for ad targeting, but 'currently' and 'explicitly' carry significant caveats in Meta's history.

Embir has no advertising model. Revenue comes from a future transparent freemium subscription. Your dating preferences, your conversations, and your matches are not shared with advertisers — because there are no advertisers. The business model doesn't require surveillance.
highlights: No Facebook account required — no social graph exposure
No ad-targeting model — your data isn't the product

sections.title: Compatibility vs social graph
sections.content:
Facebook Dating uses your social network as a compatibility signal — friends of friends, shared groups, common events. This can surface relevant people but also creates social awkwardness: what happens when you match with someone in your book club, and things don't work out?

Embir uses anonymous compatibility signals: orientation, intentions, lifestyle preferences, and values. You control what you share. There are no mutual friends to create social obligations or post-rejection awkwardness. Dating is separated from your social life by design.
highlights: Social graph completely separate from dating activity
Compatibility based on stated preferences, not social proximity

verdict:
Facebook Dating is convenient if you're already in the Facebook ecosystem and comfortable with Meta's data practices. The social graph integration can surface genuinely relevant people.

Embir is the better choice if privacy matters to you, if you want verified profiles and reporting and blocking, and if you'd rather your dating life stay separate from your social media footprint.
ctaText: Private, verified, free — dating without the data trade-off

faq.q: Does Embir share data with social networks?
faq.a: No. Embir is a standalone platform with no Facebook, Instagram, or social media integration. Your dating activity stays private.

faq.q: Is Embir free like Facebook Dating?
faq.a: Yes. Core connection features are free for everyone. The difference is the business model: Facebook's 'free' monetizes your data. Embir's transparent optional services funds safety and moderation directly.

faq.q: How does Embir verify profiles without a social media account?
faq.a: Selfie verification — a quick photo check at signup that confirms you're a real person. Reports are recorded for review, and blocking is immediate.

faq.q: Is Embir inclusive for LGBTQ+ users?
faq.a: Yes. Every orientation is supported by design — gay, lesbian, bi, pan, queer, trans, non-binary. No Facebook orientation settings required.

faq.q: Can I see who liked me on Embir for free?
faq.a: for core connection features, core discovery features are free. Full visibility into who interacted with your profile is part of the features available at launch.
